checks.a_analysis: The output faithfully represents the text from the source document without inventing new paragraphs, headings, or body text. All headings and table entries correspond to the content found in the original agenda.
There are no fabricated phone numbers, emails, or URLs in the output. While a URL is missing (see C5), the output does not contain any incorrect or hallucinated contact data.
The single-page document is fully represented in the output structure, including the header, agenda table, meeting dates, website link section, and committee reports table.

Both tables in the document (Agenda and Committee Reports) are accurately converted with the correct number of rows. The Agenda table has 9 rows (header + 8 items) and the Committee Reports table has 6 rows, matching the source exactly.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The URL 'https://it.nc.gov/gicc' is missing from the output paragraph that introduces the GICC Website. The paragraph element exists but is truncated after the colon, omitting the actual link which is the primary information of that section.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Aside from the missing URL identified in C5, all text including names, times, and committee titles are accurately transcribed with no meaning-altering changes.

checks.a_analysis: The output JSON represents each text block from the source document exactly once. There are no repeated sections, paragraphs, or lists in the conversion.
The heading hierarchy follows a logical structure starting from H1 (Council name), H2 (Agenda title), and H3 (Meeting Dates and Committee Reports). There are no skipped levels or incorrect nestings.

The second table (Committee Reports, element index 9) incorrectly identifies its first data row ('Statewide Mapping Advisory' and 'Paul Badr') as a header row ('is_header_row': true). In the source, this is clearly the first item in a list of reports, and no column headers exist for this section. Additionally, the first column contains bullet points that were merged into the text strings.
checks.b_evidence_source: The list of reports starting with '• Statewide Mapping Advisory' and 'Paul Badr'.

checks.a_analysis: The NCGICC logo, which appears at the very top of the source document as a primary branding element/header, is assigned a reading order of 10, placing it at the very end of the document after the committee reports. This is a material divergence from the intended top-to-bottom information flow.
checks.b_evidence_source: NCGICC logo at the top center of the page.

checks.a_analysis: The document contains two clear list structures that are not preserved. First, the sub-list under Agenda item 4 (a, b, c) is flattened into a single block of text within a table cell. Second, the bulleted list of Committee Reports is converted into a table. While the table preserves the association between names, the semantic structural element of a list with markers is lost.
checks.b_evidence_source:
a. Management and Operations Committee
b. Federal Interagency Committee
c. Technical Advisory Committee
checks.b_evidence_output:
"text": "4. Committee Bylaws Updates [Council Vote Required]
a. Management and Operations Committee
b. Federal Interagency Committee
c. Technical Advisory Committee"

checks.a_analysis: The URL 'https://it.nc.gov/gicc' which is explicitly written in the source document is missing from the output. The paragraph element intended to contain it only includes the label 'GICC Website: ', failing to provide the actual link or text for navigation.
checks.b_evidence_location: Paragraph after 'Remaining 2021 GICC Quarterly Meeting Dates', element index 6

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains a visual organization consistent with the source PDF. The title, subtitle, meeting information, agenda table, and committee reports are presented in the same logical order. While the NCGICC logo has been moved from the header (PDF) to the footer area (HTML), this is a standard layout adjustment for web accessibility and does not disrupt the visual flow of information. The committee reports, which were a bulleted list with right-aligned text in the PDF, are appropriately rendered as a table in HTML to preserve their visual and logical relationship.

The main agenda table is rendered correctly with clearly defined columns and rows that match the PDF content. The committee reports section is also rendered as a table, which is an effective visual representation of the original layout. There are no signs of overlapping text, missing borders that would impede understanding, or misaligned data.

The NCGICC logo, which is the only meaningful image in the source PDF, is present and correctly rendered in the HTML output (appearing at the bottom of the page). There are no broken image placeholders or missing meaningful graphics.

There is no evidence of duplicated body content (paragraphs, tables, or sections) in the HTML rendering. The presence of document titles and section headings in both the 'Contents' sidebar and the main body is an intentional accessible design feature for navigation and is not considered a duplication defect.
